refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure teleoperation communication latency p95 ≤ 150 ms end-to-end to enable safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR specifies teleoperation latency compatibility with safe remote operation. Refining it to a measurable latency threshold (p95 ≤ 150 ms) aligns with acceptance criteria and safety criticality.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure end-to-end latency under agreed network conditions using telemetry data during teleoperation sessions and verify p95 latency ≤ 150 ms.
sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency in the network link between operator and vehicle.
FR2: Ensure real-time processing and forwarding of teleoperation commands and telemetry data.
FR3: Monitor and report latency metrics to safety services for alerting.

candidate_dps: DP1: Use optimized network protocols and QoS prioritization for teleoperation data.
DP2: Implement edge computing nodes near vehicle to reduce processing delay.
DP3: Design telemetry and tasking data pipelines with minimal buffering and efficient serialization.
DP4: Integrate latency monitoring and adaptive control to maintain latency within threshold.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Use optimized network protocols and QoS prioritization for teleoperation data.

potential_sources: Coupling between telemetry data processing and network link optimization.
Dependency on safety services for latency monitoring alerts.
coupling_check.notes: DP1 logically isolates network communication optimization, minimizing coupling with other DPs. Interfaces with Safety Services for alerts are well-defined.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions meet agreed baseline for latency.
Risk that network congestion or failures could violate latency requirements.
Assumes telemetry and tasking data volumes remain within optimized protocol capacity.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider adding DP4 (latency monitoring and adaptive control) in next decomposition level to handle dynamic network conditions.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for teleoperation communication, including network protocol handling, telemetry and tasking data processing.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network connection between Teleoperation module and Vehicle module for command and telemetry exchange.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Telemetry data sent from Vehicle to Teleoperation for latency measurement and control.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Teleoperation commands received from operator or NGHE Solution to Vehicle.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Latency alerts sent to Safety Services for safety monitoring.

module_containment_scores.notes: High-level coordination but not directly responsible for latency optimization.
Processes telemetry and tasking data but network protocol optimization is outside scope.
Primary module for network protocol and latency management; best containment.
Receives alerts but does not implement latency control.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning DP1 to Teleoperation module centralizes network protocol optimization and latency management, minimizing diffusion and improving ownership clarity.
containment_risks: Potential latency impact from Vehicle processing delays outside Teleoperation control.
Dependency on network infrastructure quality beyond module scope.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Review Vehicle module processing delays in next level decomposition to ensure latency targets are met.

fr_dp_gate.reason: FR refinement is measurable and aligns with safety criticality; selected DP logically addresses the refined FR with clear assumptions.

diffusion_risks: Some latency sources may diffuse into Vehicle module processing delays.
Network infrastructure variability outside Teleoperation module control.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module shows highest containment score and clear interface boundaries, supporting good architectural packaging.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Latency can be verified end-to-end using telemetry and network monitoring tools across assigned modules.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibility for latency optimization to Teleoperation module with defined interfaces to Vehicle and Safety Services.

recommended_revisions: Add sub-FRs and DPs for Vehicle processing latency and adaptive latency control in next decomposition level.
Clarify network baseline assumptions and monitoring in Safety Services.

decision.justification: While the current decomposition is sound, adding sub-FRs and DPs for Vehicle processing latency and adaptive control will improve completeness and risk mitigation.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency in the network link between operator and vehicle.
FR2: Ensure real-time processing and forwarding of teleoperation commands and telemetry data.
FR3: Monitor and report latency metrics to safety services for alerting.

revise.why: To explicitly capture Vehicle processing latency and adaptive latency control mechanisms for comprehensive latency management.

assumptions: Telemetry and tasking data volumes remain within optimized protocol capacity.
open_risks: Network congestion or failures violating latency requirements.
evidence_needed: Latency measurement data under operational conditions.
